body: TUNGSTEN 9.43 SLS(21) delivered -- full end-to-end correctness pass across all 53,911 lines of SLS(20), split into 8 parallel independent reviews covering the whole file start to finish. Fixed all 20 findings across three severity tiers (8 critical/certain-to-fire, 7 high/conditional, remainder lower-severity + dead-code hygiene). Compiled clean via local MetaEditor: 0 errors, 1 warning -- and that warning (sesMap flow-analysis false positive) is identical in a fresh compile of the untouched SLS(20) baseline, so zero regressions introduced. Highlights: (1) OnTick re-entrancy guard never reset on the Ghost-mode/Discovery-mode exit paths -- froze ALL trade management permanently the first time the drawdown circuit-breaker did its job. (2) ProgressiveRelaxation leaked its lock on the normal no-recal-needed path -- disabled deep recalibration AND slingshot drawdown-recovery after the very first tick. (3) RecordTradeResult's entire loss-side learning branch (component EMA, online gradient, OWA, PLDS) was nested inside if(won) -- every loss-adaptive subsystem in the file had literally never seen a loss. (4) Ghost-mode long-side stop-loss hits were scored as WINS due to an &&/?: operator-precedence bug -- this directly feeds the gate that decides whether it's safe to resume real trading after a drawdown. (5) Two dangling-if(!g_CalibSilent) with no braces silently skipped whole calibration phases (3A, 3B.6) in normal production/silent mode. (6) A reversed-direction lookahead loop in the Phase 3B.8 rescan corrupted live regime thresholds using unrelated decades-old bars instead of the real forward outcome. Every single fix is marked inline with an 'SLS21 FIX' comment explaining the defect and the concrete failure scenario, so they're greppable later. NOT addressed in this pass, by design: calibration methodology itself -- your instruction was fix bugs now, revisit methodology as a separate follow-up, so this build only corrects implementation defects in the EXISTING methodology and does not redesign it. That follow-up is still open.
